summary_one_line: An unterminated `<!--` comment whose third decoded char is end-of-input (cur==0), reached under a comment-specific call context

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must contain a comment opener `<!--` (bytes 3c 21 2d 2d) that runs into a short, unterminated comment so that in xmlParseCommentComplex the third decoded char `cur = CUR_CHAR(l)` is 0 (end of input), forcing `if (cur == 0)` true and `goto not_terminated`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry `<!--` followed by long runs of `-` dashes and truncated tails (e.g. seed bytes `3c 21 2d 2d 2d ...`), and the per-role overlay marks line 4847 `goto not_terminated` and the not_terminated label (4920-4924) as winner-only [W], showing the winner repeatedly hits the cur==0 true side (W T=23 at L4846).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry DOCTYPE/DTD-heavy structure (e.g. `DOCTYPE`, `.dtd`, `<!ELEM`) so their comments terminate or never reach the third-char-EOF state; at the blocker L4846 the loser shows T=0 F=40, never taking the cur==0 true side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the xmlParseCommentComplex cur==0 edge is recorded as a distinct (call-context, edge) bucket reached only via the comment-parse path; this new-context coverage rewards seeds that drive an unterminated comment to EOF, retaining and refining them, whereas naive collapses the same edge into one already-seen bucket and never schedules energy toward the truncated-comment state.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the cur==0 test at parser.c:4846 whose globally-blocked side is the true branch leading to not_terminated.
The true side of the blocker performs goto not_terminated and is winner-only in the per-role overlay.
Winner-resolving seeds contain a `<!--` comment opener followed by long dash runs.
Loser-blocking seeds contain DOCTYPE/DTD structure rather than truncated comments.
At the blocker line the winner takes the cur==0 true side while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4846  T=23 F=6  T=0 F=40  if (cur == 0)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winning fuzzer is naive_ctx and the losing fuzzer is naive, differing by the ctx_coverage technique.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is a new (call-context, edge) bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: A new coverage bucket for naive_ctx is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply. The winner seeds' mutation_op chains (e.g. WordInterestingMutator; BytesDeleteMutator,ByteInterestingMutator,BitFlipMutator,...) are pure havoc/token ops with no dash rows, consistent with the MECHANISM CONTEXT note that naive_ctx uses 'havoc/token names only; no ParentInfo-only / dash rows' and 'No I2SRandReplace, no CMP_MAP'.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with an unterminated `<!--` comment ending at EOF in which plain naive resolves the cur==0 true branch as often as naive_ctx (no context-coverage advantage), showing the divergence is not driven by context-sensitive edge indexing.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that context-indexing specifically (not just havoc luck or differing seed scheduling) is what lets naive_ctx retain the truncated-comment state is inferential, since both fuzzers share the identical havoc/token mutator stack and only the feedback granularity differs.
